name: scroll-area
description: A scrollable viewport with optional overlay scrollbar rails (shadcn Scroll Area parity). Two modes: overlay=false uses the browser's native scrollbar (zero JS, CSP-clean, styled via token-driven CSS); overlay=true hides the native scrollbar and renders custom overlay rails driven by the scroll-area.enhancer.ts (thumb sizing via ResizeObserver, thumb drag via pointer events, aria-valuenow live updates). The three visibility policies (hover/always/scroll) are driven by CSS selectors on data-scrolling and data-pointer-over attributes toggled by the enhancer. Orientation: vertical / horizontal / both. The viewport carries tabindex=0 (keyboard-scrollable) and optionally role=region + aria-label for a named landmark. Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012): overlay=false ships zero JS.
docs: Copies scroll-area.jte and scroll-area.css to your project. Import the CSS alongside the template. When overlay=false (default) the native scrollbar is used: no JS, CSP-clean, token-styled. When overlay=true the native scrollbar is hidden (lv-scrollbar-hide class) and custom overlay rails are activated by the scroll-area.enhancer.ts (install it once via installScrollArea(runtime) in your main.ts). Rail visibility is controlled by data-type (hover/always/scroll) via CSS selectors in scroll-area.css; the enhancer sets data-scrolling and data-pointer-over on the root. Thumb drag is handled via pointer events with setPointerCapture. TOKENS_NEEDED (not yet in lievit-tokens.css): --lv-z-scrollbar:10, --lv-color-scrollbar-thumb, --lv-color-scrollbar-thumb-active, --lv-color-scrollbar-track (currently falling back to --lv-color-border / --lv-color-border-hover).
